subject: Calculus
title: Runner's Distance Tangent
brief: A runner's distance from the start is modeled by s(t)=t^2 on 0 <= t <= 4. Draw the curve and the tangent at t=2 to represent instantaneous velocity.

subject: Calculus
title: Average Rate Of Cooling
brief: A cup of tea cools according to a decreasing curve T(t). Show a secant line from t=1 to t=4 to represent average rate of change.

subject: Calculus
title: Water Flow Accumulation
brief: Water flows into a tank at rate r(t). Draw a rate curve and shade the area from t=2 to t=6 to show total water added.

subject: Calculus
title: Store Traffic Estimate
brief: A store estimates customer traffic using left endpoint rectangles under a rate curve. Show four left rectangles under an increasing curve.

subject: Calculus
title: Fencing A Pen
brief: A farmer uses 80 m of fencing to enclose a rectangular pen along a barn wall. Draw the rectangle, barn wall, and label the variable sides for optimization.

subject: Calculus
title: Sliding Ladder
brief: A 10 ft ladder leans against a wall while its base slides away. Draw the right triangle with the wall, floor, ladder, and changing distances x and y.

subject: Advanced Functions
title: Ball Height Parabola
brief: A ball is thrown upward and its height follows a parabola. Sketch the height-time graph, mark the vertex as maximum height, and show the ground intercepts.

subject: Advanced Functions
title: Bacteria Growth
brief: A bacteria culture doubles every hour. Sketch an exponential growth curve with a horizontal asymptote and label time and population.

subject: Advanced Functions
title: Sound Level Log Model
brief: Sound intensity is modeled with a logarithmic function. Sketch a log curve with its vertical asymptote and label intensity on the x-axis.

subject: Advanced Functions
title: Tide Height
brief: The height of a tide varies sinusoidally over a day. Sketch a sinusoid and mark amplitude, period, and midline.

subject: Advanced Functions
title: Medicine Concentration
brief: A medicine concentration model has a vertical asymptote and a horizontal asymptote. Sketch a rational function that shows both asymptotes.

subject: Advanced Functions
title: Taxi Fare Piecewise
brief: A taxi fare has one rate for the first kilometer and another rate after that. Sketch a piecewise linear fare graph with an open and closed endpoint at the break.

subject: Calculus and Vectors
title: Distance Across A Lake
brief: Two survey points A and B are 120 m apart. A tree C is seen from A and B with included angle A = 42 degrees. Draw triangle ABC for a law of cosines setup.

subject: Calculus and Vectors
title: Tower From Two Angles
brief: In triangle ABC, angle A = 38 degrees, angle B = 71 degrees, and side AB = 45 m. Draw a triangle for a sine law setup.

subject: Calculus and Vectors
title: Two-Leg Hike Bearing
brief: A hiker walks 6 km on a bearing of 040 degrees, then 9 km on a bearing of 115 degrees. Draw the two travel vectors and the displacement from the start.

subject: Calculus and Vectors
title: Tree Height
brief: A surveyor stands 18 m from a tree and measures an angle of elevation of 32 degrees to the top. Draw the right triangle with distance, height, and angle.

subject: Geometry
title: Pizza Sector
brief: A pizza slice is a sector with radius 10 cm and central angle 60 degrees. Draw the sector and label the radius and central angle.

subject: Calculus and Vectors
title: Airplane Wind
brief: An airplane has airspeed 500 km/h on a bearing N30E. Wind from the West is 80 km/h. Draw the air velocity, wind velocity, and ground velocity resultant.

subject: Calculus and Vectors
title: Boat Crossing River
brief: A boat heads directly across a river at 4 m/s while the current flows downstream at 3 m/s. Draw the velocity vectors and the resultant path.

subject: Calculus and Vectors
title: Two Forces On A Crate
brief: Two forces F1 = 20 N and F2 = 30 N act on a crate with an angle of 110 degrees between them. Draw the resultant force vector.

subject: Calculus and Vectors
title: Projection Onto A Ramp
brief: A force vector is applied near a ramp. Show the projection of vector u onto vector v along the ramp and the perpendicular drop.

subject: Calculus and Vectors
title: Median Vector Proof
brief: In triangle ABC, M is the midpoint of side BC. Draw vectors AB, AC, and AM to support the proof AB + AC = 2AM.

subject: Vectors / Linear Algebra
title: Drone Displacement
brief: A drone moves 2 km east, 1.5 km north, and 1 km upward. Draw a 3D vector with x, y, and z components.

subject: Linear Algebra
title: Matrix Transformation
brief: A matrix transforms the unit square into a slanted parallelogram. Draw the original unit square and the transformed parallelogram with basis vectors.

subject: Vectors / Linear Algebra
title: Plane Normal
brief: A plane in 3D has normal vector n. Draw the plane and a normal vector perpendicular to it.

subject: Vectors / Linear Algebra
title: Orthogonal Signals
brief: Two signal vectors are orthogonal. Draw two perpendicular vectors with a right angle marker.

subject: Data Management
title: Quiz Score Histogram
brief: A class records grouped quiz scores. Draw a histogram for grouped frequency data.

subject: Data Management
title: Sprint Times Boxplot
brief: A coach summarizes sprint times with a five-number summary. Draw a box-and-whisker plot.

subject: Data Management
title: Advertising And Sales
brief: A store compares advertising spending and sales revenue. Draw a scatter plot with a positive trend line.

subject: Data Management
title: Bottle Fill Amounts
brief: Bottle fill amounts are approximately normal. Draw a normal curve and shade within one standard deviation of the mean.

subject: Data Management
title: Two-Stage Defective Test
brief: A factory part is selected, then tested. Draw a two-stage probability tree with branch probabilities for pass/fail outcomes.

subject: Data Management
title: Students In Clubs
brief: Some students join music club, some join robotics club, and some join both. Draw a two-set Venn diagram.

subject: Data Management
title: Committee Selection
brief: A committee is selected from a group of students. Draw a simple branching or grouping diagram to represent combinations without listing every outcome.

subject: Discrete Math
title: Shortest Path Network
brief: A delivery driver compares routes between five towns connected by roads. Draw a weighted network graph with nodes and edge weights.

subject: Number Theory
title: Modulo Clock
brief: Show arithmetic modulo 12 on a clock diagram. Mark starting at 9 and moving forward 5 hours.

subject: Advanced Functions
title: Arithmetic Sequence Staircase
brief: A staircase has 3 blocks on the first step, 5 on the second, and 7 on the third. Draw the first few terms as a growing arithmetic pattern.

subject: Advanced Functions
title: Compound Interest Growth
brief: An investment grows by 6 percent each year. Sketch an exponential growth graph with time on the x-axis and account value on the y-axis.
